title: Enumeration Methodology Flowchart
when_to_use: During the enumeration phase of any assessment

steps.title: Initial Port Scan
steps.tip: This gives you the service map. Every subsequent step branches from these results

steps.title: Branch by Service
steps.instruction: For each open port, branch to service-specific enumeration:
steps.tip: Use a systematic approach — go port by port, never skip one

steps.title: HTTP/HTTPS Enumeration
steps.instruction: HTTP/HTTPS (80/443): run Nikto, Gobuster, check robots.txt
steps.tip: Try multiple wordlists with Gobuster. Check both / and common app paths (/api, /admin, /wp-admin)

steps.title: SMB Enumeration
steps.tip: Anonymous access is common. Check for readable shares, user lists, and policy information

steps.title: FTP Enumeration
steps.instruction: FTP (21): check anonymous login, list files
steps.tip: Try anonymous:anonymous login first. If it works, list and download everything accessible

steps.title: SSH Enumeration
steps.instruction: SSH (22): note version, check for known CVEs
steps.tip: Old SSH versions may be vulnerable. Note the version and cross-reference with CVE databases

steps.title: DNS Enumeration
steps.instruction: DNS (53): attempt zone transfer with dig axfr
steps.tip: Zone transfers expose all DNS records. Rarely possible but always worth trying

steps.title: Document Everything
steps.instruction: Document all findings per port per host in a structured table
steps.tip: Create a matrix: hosts × ports × findings. This becomes the foundation of your report
